document.summary: Jim Robertson serves as the Deputy Assistant Secretary of War for Global Investment and Economic Security, advising on defense industrial base security and related national security matters. He has extensive experience in national security policy and international relations, having worked on the House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence and at the United States Mission to the United Nations. Additionally, he has a military background as a noncommissioned Officer in the U.S. Army Reserve.
key_takeaways: Current role: Deputy Assistant Secretary of War for Global Investment and Economic Security.
Advises on defense industrial base security and foreign investment reviews.
Former Professional Staff Member on the House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence.
Served as Chief of Staff at the U.S. Mission to the United Nations.
Noncommissioned Officer in the U.S. Army Reserve with domestic and overseas service.
Holds an MBA and is pursuing further military education.
document.why_it_matters: Jim Robertson's role is crucial for ensuring the security of the defense industrial base and managing foreign investments, which are vital for national security. His extensive background in intelligence and military service enhances his capability to address complex security challenges.
